description: Curated benchmark candidates for Tasklog re-entry evaluation. The current README sample uses runnable current-format work records, while legacy CodeWebway cases are tracked here as normalization targets for a broader future sample.
selection_rules: Prefer real work with clear scope, status, and follow-up over synthetic or toy tasks.
Favor diverse case shapes: active incidents, done work, artifact-heavy work, UX/frontend work, auth flow fixes, and runtime debugging.
Exclude sparse works with no logs or benchmark-maintenance work that would bias the public sample.

cases.title: Fix terminal start failure on fleet machine
cases.notes: Strong active incident case with clear scope and concrete follow-up.

cases.title: Push current GraphCode changes to GitHub
cases.notes: Runnable current-format case, but less representative than product-facing resume work.

cases.title: Audit CodeWebway GitHub release visibility and public-readiness
cases.notes: No logs or artifacts yet; too sparse to score re-entry meaningfully.

cases.title: Audit tasklog-mcp GitHub release visibility and public-readiness
cases.notes: Useful lightweight active case, but not as strong as code-change or incident work.

cases.title: Publish work-first Tasklog model and README
cases.notes: Good README/product-positioning case, but still documentation-heavy.

cases.title: Implement tasklog follow-up docs and attachment fixes
cases.notes: Solid active work with explicit scope and multiple session logs.

cases.title: Add closed-work re-entry summaries to Tasklog
cases.notes: Best current-format closed-work case because it includes summary-first artifacts.

cases.title: Address re-entry summary review feedback
cases.notes: Useful done case, though the work shape is narrow.

cases.title: Address list_works consolidated summary review follow-up
cases.notes: Helpful for done-work re-entry coverage, but thematically close to other Tasklog review work.

cases.title: Add re-entry anti-goals to Tasklog docs
cases.notes: Small documentation case that remains valid but does not expand workflow diversity much.

cases.title: Hide consolidated recent logs from default work context
cases.notes: Good done-work case tied to behavior changes, but still within the same feature cluster.

cases.title: Document summary consolidation policy for tasklog
cases.notes: Policy/documentation case that is useful for quantity but not variety.

cases.title: Rewrite Tasklog README for GitHub clarity
cases.notes: Representative finished documentation work with a clear before/after outcome.

cases.title: Push latest Tasklog re-entry summary changes
cases.notes: No logs or artifacts yet; too sparse for a reliable benchmark case.

cases.title: Push committed Tasklog re-entry summary changes
cases.notes: Runnable current-format case, but closer to release housekeeping than product work.

cases.title: Update agent-tooling-playbook for new Tasklog version
cases.notes: Cross-repo documentation migration case with clean scope.

cases.title: Measure Tasklog re-entry value
cases.notes: Benchmark-maintenance work should stay out of the public benchmark sample.

cases.title: Terminal frontend recovery plus Thai input fix
cases.notes: High-value real product case that broadens the sample beyond Tasklog's own repo.
cases.normalization_needed: Map this log cluster into a synthetic work record with explicit scope, latest summary, and follow-up state.
